context: Evaluates the implementation of `make_key_check` and `verify_key_check` functions that enable password validation by encrypting a fixed sentinel string. On vault creation, `make_key_check` produces storable ciphertext. On vault unlock, `verify_key_check` confirms that the candidate master key matches the stored material. These functions underpin the vsyncnotes zero-knowledge password validation scheme.

checklist.name: Sentinel string usage
checklist.description: Uses the exact sentinel string `"vsyncnotes-v1-key-check"` as the plaintext that is encrypted by `make_key_check` and matched by `verify_key_check`.

checklist.name: make_key_check output
checklist.description: `make_key_check` encrypts the sentinel with the provided 32-byte key using AES-256-GCM and returns a tuple of (base64-encoded ciphertext, base64-encoded nonce) suitable for storage.

checklist.name: verify_key_check correctness
checklist.description: `verify_key_check` returns `true` when given the ciphertext and nonce produced by `make_key_check` with the same key, and `false` when given a different key or corrupted ciphertext.

checklist.name: AES-256-GCM primitives
checklist.description: Delegates the actual encryption and decryption to the `encrypt()` and `decrypt()` functions from the vsyncnotes crypto module rather than re-implementing cipher operations.

checklist.name: Error propagation
checklist.description: Returns `Result<..., anyhow::Error>` and propagates errors from the underlying encrypt/decrypt operations rather than panicking or swallowing errors.
